releases.headline: `MIGRATING.md` generator scans `deprecate()` calls in `lib/`

items.title: MIGRATING.md generator
items.body: A new generator walks every `deprecate()` call site in `lib/` and emits `MIGRATING.md` so operators always see the current deprecation timeline without hand-editing the doc.

releases.headline: `LTS-CALENDAR.md` publishes the major-cadence and algorithm posture

items.title: Public LTS calendar
items.body: `LTS-CALENDAR.md` ships the framework's major-version cadence and the PQC-first algorithm posture so operators can plan upgrades and crypto migration windows against a published timeline.

releases.headline: Bundled pure-JS mTLS engine plus vendor-update tooling

items.title: Pure-JS mTLS engine bundled
items.body: A pure-JavaScript mTLS engine ships in the framework so operators no longer pull a native-crypto dependency for client-certificate authentication.

items.title: `vendor-update.sh` helper
items.body: A vendor-refresh script lands alongside the engine so refreshing the vendored crypto sources is a single command instead of a hand-edit walk.

items.title: CHANGELOG and thanks page
items.body: The repository now carries a structured `CHANGELOG.md` and a thanks page acknowledging upstream contributors whose work the vendored stack composes.

releases.headline: Prepack guard refuses to publish any path that matches a `.gitignore` rule

items.title: Prepack gitignore cross-check
items.body: A prepack guard now refuses to publish any path the repository's `.gitignore` would have excluded from version control, so build artefacts, `.env` files, and other sensitive bytes can't accidentally ride into the npm tarball.

releases.headline: Wiki home page gains a logo hero and neon-magenta brand accents

items.title: Wiki home hero
items.body: The wiki home page now opens with a logo hero and neon-magenta brand accents so the documentation surface visually matches the rest of the framework's identity.

releases.headline: Rewrite api-snapshot wiki section in plain operator voice and fix `SECURITY.md` flag

items.title: api-snapshot wiki section rewritten
items.body: The api-snapshot section of the wiki is rewritten in plain operator voice so the surface description matches what operators actually use the snapshot for, rather than internal narrative.

items.title: `SECURITY.md` flag
items.body: A flag in `SECURITY.md` that referenced the wrong primitive name is corrected so the operator hardening checklist points at the right configuration surface.

releases.headline: Doc sweep folding the mtls CLI references and filling the api-snapshot wiki gap

items.title: mtls CLI references folded into the docs
items.body: Operator-facing docs now reference the `blamejs mtls` CLI introduced in the previous release so the documentation surface and the shipped CLI surface match.

items.title: api-snapshot wiki gap filled
items.body: The wiki gains an api-snapshot page so operators have an in-tree reference for the snapshot file used by the surface-stability check.

releases.headline: `blamejs mtls` CLI plus framework posture fix on cert fingerprinting

items.title: `blamejs mtls` CLI
items.body: A new `blamejs mtls` CLI exposes `status`, `show-cert`, `init`, `issue`, and `issue-p12` subcommands so operators manage the mTLS trust store, issue client certificates, and export PKCS#12 bundles without writing custom scripts.

items.title: Cert fingerprinting posture
items.body: The framework's certificate fingerprinting posture is corrected so the surface aligns with the rest of the PQC-first hashing defaults.

releases.headline: Defensive `NPM_TOKEN` check in the npm-publish workflow plus committed `bin/blamejs.js` executable bit

items.title: `NPM_TOKEN` defensive check
items.body: The npm-publish workflow now refuses to run when `NPM_TOKEN` is missing instead of surfacing a confusing downstream npm error, so failures fail fast with a clear cause.

items.title: `bin/blamejs.js` executable bit
items.body: The executable bit on `bin/blamejs.js` is committed to the tree so the CLI is directly runnable after install without a post-install chmod.

releases.headline: Wiki binds to `0.0.0.0` by default so `docker -p` forwarding reaches the listener

items.title: Wiki default bind address
items.body: The wiki server now binds to `0.0.0.0` by default so `docker run -p` port forwarding actually reaches the listener; the previous loopback-only bind made the published port appear unreachable from outside the container.

releases.headline: `npm-publish` workflow plus `Dockerfile` data-skeleton directory

items.title: npm-publish workflow
items.body: A new GitHub Actions workflow publishes the framework to npm on tag push so releases land on the registry without a manual `npm publish` step.

items.title: Dockerfile data-skeleton directory
items.body: The container image now pre-creates the data-directory skeleton so the framework's sealed-storage and audit primitives have writable paths on first boot under `--cap-drop ALL`.

releases.headline: `Dockerfile` switches runtime `--chown` from name to numeric UID `65532:65532`

items.title: Numeric UID for runtime chown
items.body: The runtime container now uses the numeric UID `65532:65532` for `--chown` instead of a username, so the image runs under Kubernetes `runAsNonRoot` enforcement and other admission controllers that refuse images whose user can't be resolved to a numeric ID at admission time.

releases.headline: `Dockerfile` pre-builds `public/dist` in the deps stage so the runtime container boots cleanly under `--cap-drop ALL`

items.title: `public/dist` pre-built in deps stage
items.body: The asset build moves into the deps stage so `public/dist` exists in the runtime image before boot; the previous layout tried to write build output at runtime, which fails the moment the container drops all capabilities.

releases.headline: Operator-facing env-var surface, GitHub-side ops docs, and contribution standards

items.title: Operator-facing env-var surface
items.body: The full list of operator-tunable environment variables is now documented so operators can configure the framework without reading source.

items.title: GitHub-side ops docs
items.body: The repository ships ops documentation for the GitHub-side surface (workflows, branch protection, container registry usage) so collaborators have a written reference.

items.title: Contribution standards
items.body: Contribution standards land in the repository so external contributors have a published baseline for PR style, signed commits, and the release workflow.

releases.headline: Env-var surface documented across `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.prod`, `DEPLOY.md`, and `README`; smoke-test GHCR login fix

items.title: Env-var surface documented
items.body: The operator-tunable environment variables are documented in `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.prod`, `DEPLOY.md`, and `README` so the same surface is visible from every operator entry point.

items.title: Smoke-test GHCR login
items.body: The smoke-test workflow's GHCR login step is corrected so the post-publish smoke run can pull the just-built image.

releases.headline: CI and release-container workflows gain structured output, multi-arch builds, cosign signing, and a post-publish smoke run

items.title: Structured workflow output
items.body: CI workflows now emit structured output so failures surface the failing step and command instead of a free-form log dump.

items.title: Multi-arch container builds
items.body: The release-container workflow now builds multi-architecture images so operators on `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64` pull from the same tag.

items.title: Cosign signing
items.body: Published container images are signed with cosign so operators can verify the signature against the workflow's OIDC identity.

items.title: Post-publish smoke run
items.body: A post-publish smoke run boots the freshly published image and exercises the basic framework boot path so registry-side regressions are caught before operators pull.

releases.headline: `_runVault` and `_runBackup` refactored onto `b.cliHelpers`

items.title: `_runVault` and `_runBackup` use `b.cliHelpers`
items.body: The `_runVault` and `_runBackup` CLI entry points are refactored to use the `b.cliHelpers` shared primitive so all three CLI commands the helper was designed for now share a single headless-app and reporter shape.

releases.headline: `blamejs api-key` CLI plus shared `b.cliHelpers` primitive

items.title: `blamejs api-key` CLI
items.body: A new `blamejs api-key` CLI manages API-key lifecycle from the command line so operators no longer need bespoke scripts to issue, rotate, or revoke keys.

items.title: `b.cliHelpers` shared primitive
items.body: A new `b.cliHelpers` primitive captures the shared headless-app boot and reporter shape used by every framework CLI so future commands compose the helper instead of reimplementing the wrapper.

releases.headline: `blamejs backup` CLI with inspect / verify / extract subcommands

items.title: `blamejs backup` CLI
items.body: A new `blamejs backup` CLI ships `inspect`, `verify`, and `extract` subcommands so operators can introspect, verify the integrity of, and selectively extract sealed backups from the command line.

items.title: Backup encryption format documented
items.body: The backup encryption format is documented so operators have a written reference for the on-disk shape and the algorithms involved.

releases.headline: Drop the Windows-flaky `audit.flush` performance assertion

items.title: Windows-flaky `audit.flush` assertion removed
items.body: A performance-budget assertion around `audit.flush` was dropping under Windows file-system jitter even when the underlying behavior was correct, so the redundant assertion is removed; the same path is covered by the audit chain's integrity test.

releases.headline: Wiki docs for the unwired primitives surface

items.title: Wiki coverage for unwired primitives
items.body: The wiki now documents the framework primitives that exist in `lib/` but had not yet been written up, so the documentation surface matches what's actually shipped.

releases.headline: `blamejs vault` CLI with seal / unseal / rotate / status subcommands

items.title: `blamejs vault` CLI
items.body: A new `blamejs vault` CLI ships `seal`, `unseal`, `rotate`, and `status` subcommands so operators control the framework's sealed-storage vault from the command line instead of writing one-off scripts around the underlying primitives.

releases.headline: `validateOpts` extended to 15 more operator-facing primitives
releases.summary: Fifteen additional operator-facing factories now reject typo'd opts at boot, bringing the total coverage to roughly 39 primitives.

items.title: Boot-time opts validation for 15 more primitives
items.body: `validateOpts` coverage now extends to `i18n`, `bundler`, `migrations`, `seeders`, `template`, `jobs`, `cookies`, `log`, `mtlsCa`, `staticServe`, `handlers`, `websocket`, `pqcGate`, `tracing`, and `metrics`, so a typo in an opt key now throws at boot instead of silently doing nothing. The remaining factories (`appShutdown`, `chainWriter`, `dev`, `errorPage`, `nonceStore`, `protocolDispatcher`, `render`, `session`, `pqcAgent`) are either internal-only or have a single required-key surface where typo guards add no value.

releases.headline: Config validation, CSRF cookie mode, HTML balance, dynamic `Cache-Control`, URL canonicalization

items.title: Config validation
items.body: A new config-validation surface refuses typo'd or out-of-range configuration values at boot so operators catch mistakes immediately.

items.title: CSRF cookie mode
items.body: A cookie-backed CSRF mode is now supported in addition to the header-token mode, so operators on stateless backends have a primitive that matches their session shape.

items.title: HTML balance check
items.body: An HTML balance check protects against mismatched tag emission from templates so rendered pages can't ship with a structural defect.

items.title: Dynamic `Cache-Control`
items.body: Dynamic `Cache-Control` selection ships so operator-rendered pages get an appropriate cache directive without per-route boilerplate.

items.title: URL canonicalization
items.body: URL canonicalization normalizes incoming request paths so middleware and routing don't see two distinct representations of the same logical resource.

releases.headline: CORS accepts `Origin: null` when `Sec-Fetch-Site` is same-origin

items.title: CORS handling of opaque `Origin: null`
items.body: Browsers send an opaque `Origin: null` on form-navigation POSTs from a page whose response sets `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer`. The CORS check now consults the Fetch-metadata `Sec-Fetch-Site` header to disambiguate: `same-origin` or `none` accepts the request, anything else stays refused. Cross-origin posts can't bypass the check because they'd carry a non-same-origin `Sec-Fetch-Site` value.

releases.headline: `Dockerfile` hotfix plus lint as a release gate

items.title: Lint promoted to release gate
items.body: Linting now blocks the release workflow so style and obvious-bug findings are caught before a publish instead of after operators pull.

items.title: `Dockerfile` hotfix
items.body: A hotfix repairs the `Dockerfile` so the container builds cleanly after the previous release's changes.

releases.headline: CI lint scans, Wolfi container base, two-step Trivy scan, and lint cleanup

items.title: CI lint scans
items.body: Lint scans run on every CI build so style and obvious-bug findings surface alongside the existing smoke gate.

items.title: Wolfi container base
items.body: The container image moves to a Wolfi base for the minimal-surface, vulnerability-tracked distro the security posture wants.

items.title: Two-step Trivy scan
items.body: Trivy now runs as a two-step scan (filesystem then image) so vulnerabilities are caught both pre-build and post-build.

items.title: Lint cleanup
items.body: The codebase is cleaned to satisfy the newly-enforced lint rules so the gate runs cleanly on every release.

releases.headline: Version bump for a hotfix container-image rebuild

items.title: Container-image rebuild
items.body: This release exists to bump the version so the container image rebuilds against the latest base layers; no framework-code changes ship in this release.

releases.headline: CI/CD: GitHub Actions for smoke and wiki e2e, GHCR container publish on tag, Caddy production deploy overlay

items.title: GitHub Actions for smoke and wiki e2e
items.body: Smoke and wiki end-to-end tests now run on every push via GitHub Actions so regressions are caught before a release tag is cut.

items.title: GHCR container publish on tag
items.body: Tagged releases now publish a container image to GHCR so operators can pull a versioned container alongside the npm tarball.

items.title: Caddy production deploy overlay
items.body: A Caddy production deploy overlay ships so operators have a known-good reverse-proxy configuration that exercises the framework's TLS and header defaults.

releases.headline: Framework correctness fixes across compression, CSP nonce, CORS, and audit
releases.summary: Four correctness fixes land together: compression now honors backpressure, `cspNonce` exposes a cacheable-render API, CORS rejects cross-origin requests that previously slipped through the same-origin path, and audit namespaces are now correctly threaded through.

items.title: Compression backpressure
items.body: The compression middleware now honors downstream backpressure, preventing unbounded memory growth when the client consumes more slowly than the encoder produces.

items.title: `cspNonce` cacheable-render API
items.body: `cspNonce` exposes a cacheable-render API so responses generated by a shared renderer can carry a per-request nonce without invalidating the rendered cache.

items.title: CORS same-origin handling
items.body: Tightens the CORS middleware's same-origin path so cross-origin requests that previously slipped through under specific header shapes are now refused.

items.title: Audit namespaces
items.body: Audit events are now correctly namespaced so per-domain audit streams stay distinct instead of folding into the global stream.

releases.headline: Wiki: Docker Compose deploy

items.title: Docker Compose deploy wiki page
items.body: New wiki page describing how to stand up the framework via Docker Compose, including the recommended service layout and the env-var surface operators need to wire.

releases.headline: Wiki: remaining four concern-group pages

items.title: Remaining concern-group wiki pages
items.body: Finishes the per-concern wiki coverage with the four remaining concern-group pages, completing the wiki navigation surface for all primitive domains.

releases.headline: Wiki: HTTP & Middleware and Crypto & Vault pages

items.title: HTTP & Middleware and Crypto & Vault wiki pages
items.body: New wiki content covering the HTTP request lifecycle / middleware concern group and the crypto / vault concern group so operators can navigate the primitive surface for those domains.

releases.headline: Wiki: Auth & Permissions and Storage & State pages

items.title: Auth & Permissions and Storage & State wiki pages
items.body: New wiki content covering the authentication / authorization concern group and the storage / state concern group (session, db, cache, vault) so operators can navigate the primitive surface for those domains.

releases.headline: Strip stale comments and template language from sources

items.title: Source comments cleaned of stale and template language
items.body: Removed leftover scaffolding comments and template prose across `lib/` so the in-source documentation reflects current behavior. No runtime behavior change.

releases.headline: Wiki: Welcome and Observability pages

items.title: Welcome and Observability wiki pages
items.body: New wiki content covering the framework entry-point and the observability concern group (metrics, tracing, audit) so operators can navigate from the landing page into the observability primitive surface.

releases.headline: Wiki bundler integration + admin editor enhancements

items.title: Wiki bundler integration + admin editor enhancements
items.body: The `examples/wiki` reference app gains bundler integration and admin editor improvements so operators evaluating the framework see the documentation surface render with the same wiring they would use in production.

releases.headline: Wiki wires `b.notify` / `b.scheduler` / `b.apiKey` / `b.webhook`

items.title: Wiki demonstrates notify / scheduler / apiKey / webhook composition
items.body: The `examples/wiki` reference app now exercises `b.notify`, `b.scheduler`, `b.apiKey`, and `b.webhook` end-to-end so operators evaluating the framework see how these primitives compose with the rest of the request lifecycle.

releases.headline: Wiki design pass

items.title: Wiki design pass
items.body: Visual and layout refinements across the `examples/wiki` reference app so the documentation surface reads cleanly and the navigation reflects the current primitive set.

releases.headline: `examples/wiki` reference app
releases.summary: The framework ships with a reference wiki application under `examples/wiki` that operators can clone, run, and read to see the framework's primitives composed into a working server.

items.title: `examples/wiki` reference app
items.body: A working wiki application built on the framework lands under `examples/wiki`. It composes the request lifecycle, the security defaults, the storage primitives, and the documentation surface so operators evaluating the framework see an end-to-end shape instead of isolated snippets.
